event: Christ in the House of the Pharisee
symbols: woman washing Jesus's feet with tears — repentance and love overflowing
Simon seated judging — religious self-righteousness contrasted with humble devotion
banquet setting — the hospitality that the Pharisee withheld but the woman exceeded
description: A Renaissance interior scene showing a banquet in an columned hall. Jesus sits at a table with the Pharisee Simon and other guests. In the foreground, a woman kneels at Jesus's feet, her long hair falling forward as she anoints them. Simon and the other guests react with surprise or disapproval. A dog sits at the lower left. The architecture features classical columns opening onto a distant landscape.
theological_description: This scene (LUK.7.36-50) dramatizes the confrontation between self-righteousness and genuine repentance. A sinful woman enters the Pharisee's house uninvited, washes Jesus's feet with her tears, dries them with her hair, and anoints them with perfume. Simon thinks: 'If this man were a prophet, he would have known who and what sort of woman this is' (LUK.7.39). Jesus responds with the parable of two debtors, concluding: 'Her sins, which are many, are forgiven — for she loved much. But he who is forgiven little, loves little' (LUK.7.47). The scene reveals that proximity to Jesus does not guarantee understanding — the Pharisee at the table misses what the sinner at his feet grasps.
